suite_note: Shared cross-language CAID conformance vectors. Each vector carries its own inline type definitions and never references the public registry. CAID carries no trust semantics: these vectors test identification only, not authorization, identity, or proof.

vectors.description: payment.release.1 example from DESIGN.md, all required fields plus optional memo

definitions.summary: Release of a payment instruction to settlement.

required_fields.name: amount
required_fields.notes: decimal string, no exponent, no leading '+', no thousands separators

required_fields.name: beneficiary_account
required_fields.notes: sha256:<lowercase hex> of the normalized account identifier; normalization stated by the issuing system of record

definitions.digest_notes: amounts never renormalized after signing; the system of record's form is canonical

vectors.description: doc.sign.1 with digest, string, timestamp, and optional integer fields

definitions.summary: Application of a signature to a document digest.

required_fields.name: document
required_fields.notes: sha256:<lowercase hex> of the document bytes

vectors.description: inventory.adjust.1 with integer, enum, array, object, and boolean fields, plus a negative integer

definitions.summary: Adjustment of on-hand inventory for a SKU.
